pattern: A run only survives while consecutive differences flip sign in lockstep (+1, -1, +1, ...), so one sweep that starts a fresh candidate the moment that pattern begins and drops it the moment it breaks finds the longest one.
hints: A qualifying run always begins with a step of exactly +1 -- so only ever consider starting a new candidate at a position where the very next value is exactly one greater.
Once started, the run must keep alternating +1, -1, +1, -1, ...; extend it one step at a time only while the next difference matches whichever sign is expected next.

The instant the expected difference is not met, the run is over -- record its length if it beats the best seen so far, and immediately try starting a new run from that same breaking position (it only succeeds if it is itself a valid +1 start).

edge_inputs.description: run of length 4 embedded among shorter ones
no valid run exists anywhere, strictly decreasing
all identical values, no valid run
long run broken briefly then a fresh shorter run begins
a +1 step followed immediately by another +1 step ends the run at length 2

stress.description: 100 random values (max length) from the allowed range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(max(size, 2), 100)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
